title: fix(gen): remove artificial fetch timeouts
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Removes the 90s/300s timeout from generic OpenAI/Portkey fetches.
- Removes manual timeout caps from Google token exchange and text image inlining.
- Removes unused timeout helper code and Azure Content Safety Promise.race wrappers.
- Keeps Tinybird ingest timeout and explicit async polling budgets.

## Tests
- npx biome check --write <changed files>
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npx vitest run test/text/genericOpenAIClient.test.ts test/text/transforms/imageUrlToBase64Transform.test.ts

gist.headline: Let the models cook
gist.blurb: We smashed the artificial clocks on our backend fetches. Complex generations now have the time they need to finish without getting cut off early.
gist.summary: Removed the artificial 90s and 300s fetch timeouts from generic OpenAI and Portkey endpoints, alongside manual caps on Google token exchanges and image inlining.
gist.impact: Long-running text and image generations will no longer fail prematurely due to internal timeout wrappers, resulting in fewer interrupted API requests.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and the monitor robot relaxing patiently while a complex process finishes. A smashed hourglass represents the removal of the artificial timeouts.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot and the chunky monitor robot relaxing in comfortable chairs, patiently watching a massive, intricate code-plant slowly bloom from a lime green (#ecf874) pot. A smashed hourglass lies on the wooden floor nearby. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background with warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, peaceful, no rushing.
